love.shortReport: Elite three-down RB with home-run burst and receiving upside. Notre Dame's bellcow — 17 TDs in 2024, 14 in 2025 prove nose for the end zone. Receiving volume trending up every year (8→28→27 catches). Also contributed 3 return TDs.
love.athleticProfile: Explosive first step, loose hips, max-speed corner-press winner. Long speed translates on tape — pulls away from DBs at the second level. 4.38 estimated, contact balance through the hole is elite.
strengths: Explosive burst through the hole — reaches top speed in two strides
Receiving upside: natural hands, routes improved every year (8→28→27 rec, added 2 receiving TDs in 2025)
Goal-line and short-yardage finishing — 48 total TDs over 3 seasons behind varying OL quality
Contact balance — bounces off arm tackles, keeps legs churning through contact
Return value: 2 KR TDs in 2024, 1 PR TD in 2025 adds hidden fantasy floor
weaknesses: Pass-pro technique still raw — leans into rushers instead of mirroring
Can be too patient on zone reads — needs to commit faster to the hole
YPC declined each year (7.9→6.9→6.6) — could signal OL dependency or workload wear
Never had 200+ carries in a season — durability over a full NFL workload unproven
love.nflFit: Three-down feature back in a zone-run scheme. Best fit: team with outside-zone DNA and a need for pass-game upside at RB.
love.comp: Alvin Kamara lite — same receiving profile, slightly less elusiveness in space
love.dynastyOutlook: Top-3 dynasty RB in this class. Locked-in three-down role day one if landing spot is reasonable. Receiving growth gives him weekly RB1 ceiling in PPR.
love.badgersFit: Perfect Badgers fit. You need a franchise RB and Love is the best in this class. Three-down profile means he never leaves the field. The receiving upside (27 catches in 2025) is gravy in PPR. At pick 1.03 you're getting a potential dynasty RB1. Don't overthink it.
love.verdict: Elite RB1 profile. Draft and never look back.

mendoza.shortReport: Gunslinger QB who exploded at Indiana in 2025 — 35 TD, 6 INT, 70.7% with 312 rush yds and 6 rushing TDs. Elite arm talent with plus mobility. The 2025 season was a legit breakout; Cal tape was merely decent.
mendoza.athleticProfile: Above-average athlete for the position. Escapes the pocket with purpose, not just scrambling — keeps eyes downfield. Rushing production climbed every year (112→198→312 yds) showing growing comfort as a runner. Not a designed-run QB but a functional scrambler who adds 5-6 rush TDs.
strengths: Elite arm strength — drives the ball 55+ yards with touch and layered accuracy
Dramatically improved decision-making at Indiana: 35 TDs vs 6 INT in 2025 (was 17-6 and 16-6 at Cal)
Off-schedule playmaker — extends plays and throws accurately on the move
Quick release — gets the ball out in 2.3s average, bails out OL issues
Rushing upside: 6 rushing TDs in 2025 gives him a weekly QB1 floor even in bad passing matchups
3 game-winning drives in 2025 — clutch gene is real
weaknesses: Still prone to hero-ball turnovers under pressure — will force one he shouldn't per game
Mechanics break down when pocket collapses — arm-only thrower off back foot
Only one elite season — Cal tape was inconsistent, 2025 could be peak not floor
Completion percentage jump (63.8%→70.7%) is suspicious — may regress with better defenders
mendoza.nflFit: Best in a spread/RPO system that maximizes arm talent and movement. Not a pure pocket guy — needs bootleg/play-action elements. Rushing TDs make him a fantasy QB1 even in down passing weeks.
mendoza.comp: Jordan Love trajectory — raw early, elite arm, needs reps. Rushing adds a Jalen Hurts-lite floor.
mendoza.dynastyOutlook: QB1 in this class for dynasty. Ceiling is a top-3 fantasy QB with the rushing floor. The 35-6 TD-INT ratio is elite regardless of competition.
mendoza.badgersFit: If you need a franchise QB, Mendoza is the pick. The rushing TDs (6 in 2025) give him a weekly floor that most pocket QBs can't match. In a dynasty league, a dual-threat QB with this arm talent is gold. He's your 1.01 if you're QB-needy. If you already have a QB, he's still worth a 1st-round pick as a trade asset.
mendoza.verdict: High-end QB1 with elite ceiling. The rushing floor seals it.

tate.shortReport: Ohio State X-receiver who broke out in 2025 with 12 TDs on 52 catches (22.2 YPR). Prototypical build with contested-catch dominance. Route tree expanded significantly under Hartline. Slow 2023 start (18 catches) is the only real concern.
tate.athleticProfile: Big, physical X-receiver with strong hands and leaping timing. Not a burner — wins with size, body positioning, and catch radius. 22.2 YPR in 2025 shows he wins downfield with positioning, not pure speed.
strengths: Contested-catch monster — wins 50/50 balls with strong hands and timing at the high point
Red-zone weapon — 12 TDs on 52 catches in 2025 (23.1% TD rate is absurd)
Route tree expanded significantly under Hartline — runs the full tree now
Strong after the catch — breaks tackles with size and lean, 22.2 YPR isn't all deep balls
Production improved every year: 18→52→52 catches, 1→4→12 TDs
weaknesses: Separation questions against elite press-man corners — can be erased by physical CBs
Doesn't have elite top-end speed — wins with size, not burst
Slow start to career (18 catches in 2023) — late bloomer, one-year TD spike risk
TD rate (23.1%) is unsustainable — expect regression to 7-8 TD range
tate.nflFit: X-receiver in a vertical passing game. Best with a QB who can throw him open on 50/50 balls. TD dependency makes him boom/bust in fantasy.
tate.comp: Mike Evans-lite — similar size/catch radius, slightly less speed and consistency
tate.dynastyOutlook: Solid WR2/3 in dynasty. TD dependency is a risk but the ceiling is a weekly WR1. The 12-TD season is his peak, not his floor.
tate.badgersFit: Tate is a premium talent at a premium position. The 12 TDs are flashy but expect 7-9 in a typical year. He's a WR2 with WR1 ceiling in PPR. At pick 1.04 he's a solid get if you went RB/QB earlier. The contested-catch skill means he's QB-proof — he'll produce even with a mediocre passer.
tate.verdict: Top-5 dynasty WR in this class. Plug and play with TD upside.

tyson.shortReport: Explosive ASU WR who overcame a 2023 knee injury. Deep-speed threat with elite after-the-catch ability. Back-to-back 1,000+ yd seasons (75-1101-10 in 2024, 71-1005-8 in 2025). Versatile alignment — plays outside and slot. Also contributed on punt returns.
tyson.athleticProfile: Twitchy athlete with elite deep speed and loose hips in breaks. The knee injury didn't rob his explosiveness — he's still a vertical threat who pulls away from DBs. YAC ability is his calling card.
strengths: Elite deep speed — stretches the field vertically, commands safety attention
After-the-catch dynamo — makes defenders miss in space, 14.7 YPR shows catch-and-run ability
High-end production post-injury: 75-1101-10 in 2024, 71-1005-8 in 2025 — no regression
Versatile alignment — plays outside and in the slot, creates mismatches
Return ability: 2 PR for 50+ yds in 2024, adds special-teams floor
weaknesses: Knee injury history in 2023 — medical flag for some teams, could limit draft capital
Can round off routes at the top of the stem — loses separation against patient corners
Concentration drops on crossers — takes eye off ball early to set up YAC
Yards per catch declined (14.7→14.2) — could indicate declining separation or defensive adjustment
tyson.nflFit: Z-receiver or slot in a spread system. Best with a QB who throws a great deep ball. The YAC ability makes him scheme-proof — he'll produce in any offense that gets him the ball in space.
tyson.comp: DJ Moore — same YAC ability and alignment flexibility, slightly less physical
tyson.dynastyOutlook: WR2/3 with weekly WR1 upside if he stays healthy. The knee is the only thing keeping him from the top tier.
tyson.badgersFit: Tyson is a high-ceiling WR who fits what the Badgers need — explosive playmaking. The knee injury is the risk, but two 1,000-yd seasons post-injury are reassuring. He's a WR2 with WR1 upside at pick 1.08. If you already grabbed Love or Mendoza, Tyson is the perfect WR1 to pair with them.
tyson.verdict: High ceiling, moderate risk. Worth the pick if you can absorb the injury floor.

lemon.shortReport: USC Biletnikoff winner. Elite route runner with suddenness out of breaks — 79-1156-11 TDs in 2025. The safest WR in this class. Production climbed every year (23→52→79 catches). Not a burner but wins with technique and hands.
lemon.athleticProfile: Twitchy, sudden athlete who wins with quickness not speed. Loose hips in breaks, no wasted movement. Smaller frame but plays bigger through technique. Not going to run by anyone but will create separation on every route.
strengths: Elite route runner — creates separation consistently against all coverage types
Biletnikoff winner in 2025: 79-1156-11 TDs — production is undeniable
Sudden out of breaks — no wasted movement at the stem top, QB-friendly target
Reliable hands — drops are rare, catches everything in his frame
Production trajectory: 23→52→79 catches, 0→6→11 TDs — every year better than the last
weaknesses: Not a burner — wins with technique, not speed. Won't take the top off NFL defenses
Smaller frame — can be knocked off routes by press coverage from bigger CBs
USC system inflated some numbers — lots of schemed touches, screens, and RPOs
YPR declined (13.7→14.7→14.6) — capped ceiling without deep-ball ability
lemon.nflFit: Slot or Z in a timing-based offense. Best with a precise QB who throws on time. Scheme-proof — his route running translates to any system.
lemon.comp: Ceedee Lamb — similar route nuance and body control, minus the elite athleticism
lemon.dynastyOutlook: Top-3 dynasty WR. Safe floor with weekly WR2 production. The Biletnikoff is a strong signal — recent winners have been hits at the next level.
lemon.badgersFit: Lemon is the safest WR pick you can make. The route running means he'll produce regardless of QB or scheme. At pick 1.20 he's a steal — he should go higher. He's your WR1 if you went RB/QB early, or a filthy WR2 if you took Tate/Tyson first. The floor is high; the ceiling is capped without deep speed.
lemon.verdict: The safest WR pick in this class. Take him and enjoy the floor.

price.shortReport: Notre Dame RB returning from a 2023 Achilles tear. Explosive when healthy — 11 TDs in 2025 on just 113 carries (6.0 YPC). Receiving upside emerging: 22-187-2TD in 2025. The burst is back but durability and workload are real questions.
price.athleticProfile: Explosive burst is his defining trait — hits the hole with urgency and reaches top speed quickly. Achilles recovery was clean; no visible limitations on tape. Smaller frame raises durability concerns over a full NFL season.
strengths: Explosive burst — hits the hole with urgency, 6.0 YPC in 2025 shows the big-play ability
Goal-line finisher — 11 TDs in 2025 on limited carries, nose for the end zone
Pass-game upside — 22-187-2TD receiving in 2025, natural hands out of the backfield
Achilles recovery was clean — no visible limitations on tape, cut ability is intact
weaknesses: Achilles injury in 2023 — long-term durability concern, re-injury risk is elevated
Limited workload — never had 120+ carries in a season, can he handle 200+?
Pass protection still developing — liabilities in blitz pickup will limit third-down snaps
YPC dropped from 6.9 to 6.0 — could be regression or OL variance
price.nflFit: Change-of-pace / committee back with RB2 upside. Best in a zone scheme that maximizes his burst. Receiving ability keeps him on the field on third downs.
price.comp: James Cook — same burst/receiving profile, slightly less elusiveness
price.dynastyOutlook: RB3/4 with weekly RB2 upside if he earns a role. Injury floor is real but the TD production is enticing.
price.badgersFit: Price is a high-upside RB2 if the medicals check out. The 11 TDs on 113 carries is elite efficiency, but he's never proven he can handle a full workload. At pick 2.32 he's worth the dart — if you already have Love, Price is a premium handcuff with standalone value. The receiving upside (22 catches in 2025) gives him PPR flex appeal.
price.verdict: High-upside RB2 if the medicals check out. Worth the dart throw.

concepcion.shortReport: Shifty slot WR who transferred from NC State to Texas A&M. Elite after the catch with sudden route running. 2023 was elite (71-839-10TD), 2025 was solid (55-661-8TD). Also contributes on punt returns (1 TD in 2023). Small frame limits his ceiling.
concepcion.athleticProfile: Twitchy slot athlete with elite short-area quickness. Makes the first man miss consistently. Small frame limits his effectiveness against press and over the middle. Not a deep threat — wins underneath and in space.
strengths: Elite YAC ability — makes the first man miss consistently, dangerous in space
Sudden route runner — creates instant separation on slants, screens, and option routes
Versatile — returns punts (1 TD in 2023) and plays gadget roles, adds hidden floor
Productive at NC State: 71-839-10 in 2023, proved it again at TAMU: 55-661-8
weaknesses: Small frame — struggles against press-man, can be erased by physical CBs
Dropped off at TAMU — 55-661-8 is fine but not the 71-839-10 he had at NCST
Not a deep threat — wins underneath, capped fantasy ceiling without big plays
Gadget/slot only — can't win on the boundary, limits snap count
concepcion.nflFit: Slot receiver in a spread offense. Best with a QB who gets the ball out quickly. Return ability gives him a roster floor even if the offense doesn't feature him.
concepcion.comp: Tank Dell — same size/suddenness profile, slightly less deep speed
concepcion.dynastyOutlook: WR4/5 with weekly WR3 upside in the right matchup. The return value adds a floor in return-yard leagues.
concepcion.badgersFit: Concepcion is a useful dynasty piece but not a building block. He's a slot-only gadget player who gives you WR3 weeks when the matchup is right. At pick 2.24 he's decent value if you already have your WR1/2. The punt return TD is a bonus in return-yard leagues. Draft for depth, not as a starter.
concepcion.verdict: Useful dynasty piece but not a building block. Draft for depth.

sadiq.shortReport: Oregon TE with elite athleticism and ascending production — 2→24→36 catches, 0→2→5 TDs. 6'6" 241 lbs former basketball player with a 34.1" vert. Raw blocker but the athletic profile is rare for the position. Red-zone mismatch waiting to happen.
sadiq.athleticProfile: 6'6" 241 lbs with a basketball background and a 34.1" vertical. Fluid mover for his size — can sink and break like a smaller athlete. Long-strider who covers ground quickly. Raw as a blocker but the movement skills are undeniable.
strengths: Elite athletic profile — former basketball player with fluid movement at 6'6" 241
Red-zone threat — 5 TDs in 2025 on 36 catches, uses his size and vert to win above the rim
Improving route runner — getting cleaner out of breaks every year, trajectory is steep
YAC ability — stiff-arms and hurdles defenders in space, not just a big target
Ascending production: 2→24→36 catches, 0→2→5 TDs — arrow pointing way up
weaknesses: Still raw as a blocker — needs significant technique work, will limit inline snaps
Only one semi-productive season — 2023 was 2 catches, still developing
Can drift on routes when the play breaks down — needs more reps
36 catches isn't dominant — volume ceiling depends on NFL role
sadiq.nflFit: Move TE in a spread offense. Best as an F-back who creates mismatches in the slot. Won't be an every-down inline TE — his value is as a jumbo slot.
sadiq.comp: Evan Engram — same athletic/positional profile, slightly less polished as a route runner
sadiq.dynastyOutlook: TE1 with weekly TE1 upside. The athleticism is rare for the position. In a weak TE class, he's the prize.
sadiq.badgersFit: Sadiq is the TE to own in this class. The 6'6" frame with basketball athleticism is a red-zone cheat code. At pick 2.16 he's a bargain for a potential TE1. If you're tired of streaming TEs, Sadiq is your exit ticket. The ascending production (2→24→36 catches) means he's just getting started.
sadiq.verdict: Top dynasty TE in this class. Take him if you need a TE.

simpson.shortReport: Alabama QB who finally got his chance in 2025 — 2,824 yds, 28 TD, 4 INT, 71.4% with 287 rush yds and 5 rushing TDs. Elite accuracy with functional mobility. Sat behind Milroe for 2 years but the 2025 tape is legit first-round quality.
simpson.athleticProfile: Functional athlete — not a designed-runner but effective scrambler (62-287-5TD in 2025). Clean mechanics, quick release. Above-average mobility for a pocket passer. The 5 rushing TDs show he can extend plays and score with his legs.
strengths: Elite accuracy — 71.4% completion rate in 2025, puts the ball in stride consistently
Outstanding TD-INT ratio — 28-4 in his first starting season is remarkable
Functional mobility — 287 rush yds and 5 TDs give him a fantasy floor beyond passing
Quick release and clean mechanics — processes the field fast, gets the ball out
Alabama pedigree — developed under Saban/DeBoer, NFL-ready preparation
weaknesses: Only one year of starting tape — 2023-24 were backup duty (29 attempts total)
Alabama talent inflation — surrounded by NFL-caliber weapons, can he elevate lesser rosters?
Arm strength is good not elite — struggles to drive the ball on deep outs
28 TDs may be peak — expect regression with fewer weapons at the next level
simpson.nflFit: Best in a West Coast / play-action system that emphasizes accuracy and timing. The rushing TDs make him a fantasy asset even if the passing volume is moderate.
simpson.comp: Brock Purdy with legs — accurate, efficient, functional mobility, not a tools freak
simpson.dynastyOutlook: QB2/3 in this class. The 28-4 ratio is elite but one-year wonders are risky. The rushing TDs (5) give him a weekly QB1 floor in fantasy.
simpson.badgersFit: Simpson is the QB2 in this class behind Mendoza. The 71.4% accuracy and 28-4 TD-INT ratio are elite, but it's one year of tape at Alabama with NFL weapons. The 5 rushing TDs are the fantasy hook — he's a QB1 with his legs even in bad passing weeks. At pick 2.13 he's worth it if you missed on Mendoza. Pair him with a RB/WR and you're set.
simpson.verdict: High-floor QB2 with QB1 upside via rushing TDs. One-year risk is real.

cooper.shortReport: Indiana WR with ascending production — 12→45→74 catches, 2→7→9 TDs. Big-play ability (18.3 YPR in 2023, 16.2 in 2024). Reliable target who grew into a go-to role. Big Ten WR of the Week x3 in 2025.
cooper.athleticProfile: Smooth athlete with good size and long speed. Wins downfield with tracking and body positioning. Not sudden in breaks but builds speed efficiently. Good after the catch with size to break arm tackles.
strengths: Ascending production: 12→45→74 catches, every year significantly better
Big-play ability — 18.3 YPR in 2023, 16.2 in 2024, averaged 12.6 in 2025 with volume
Reliable target — grew into a go-to role, earned Big Ten WR of the Week x3 in 2025
Red-zone threat — 9 TDs in 2025 on 74 catches, wins with size and positioning
weaknesses: Not sudden in breaks — separation against quick-twitch corners is a concern
Big Ten production came in a spread offense — some system inflation
YPR dropped with volume (18.3→16.2→12.6) — efficiency vs volume tradeoff
One elite year — 2023-24 were modest, could be a late bloomer or one-year wonder
cooper.nflFit: Z-receiver in a spread offense. Best with a QB who throws with anticipation. The ascending production suggests he's still improving.
cooper.comp: Michael Pittman — similar size/production trajectory, reliable but not explosive
cooper.dynastyOutlook: WR3/4 with weekly WR2 upside. The ascending production is encouraging but one elite year is a risk.
cooper.badgersFit: Cooper is a solid WR3 with WR2 upside if he continues his trajectory. At pick 2.30 he's great value — the ascending production (12→45→74 catches) is a strong signal. He's a reliable target who won't tank your week. Pair him with a WR1 like Tate or Tyson and you're set at receiver.
cooper.verdict: Ascending WR with solid floor. Worth the pick in the late 2nd.

lane.shortReport: USC red-zone specialist with a 6'4" frame — 12 TDs on 46 catches in 2024, 6 TDs on 47 catches in 2025. Massive catch radius makes him a goal-line cheat code. Production is TD-dependent; volume is modest.
strengths: 6'4" frame with massive catch radius — red-zone cheat code
12 TDs on 46 catches in 2024 (26.1% TD rate) is absurd efficiency
Improved YPR from 11.4 to 17.2 in 2025 — adding downfield ability
Wins 50/50 balls consistently — QB can just throw it up
weaknesses: TD-dependent — when he doesn't score, the floor is low
Modest volume — never had 50 catches in a season
Limited route tree — mostly go-balls, fades, and slants
TD rate will regress — 12 TDs on 46 catches is unsustainable
lane.nflFit: Red-zone specialist X-receiver. Best in an offense that needs a jump-ball target near the goal line.
lane.comp: Mike Williams (Chargers version) — big, TD-dependent, limited route tree
lane.dynastyOutlook: WR4/5 with weekly WR2 upside in TD-only formats. In PPR, the low volume caps his floor.
lane.badgersFit: Lane is a TD-or-bust WR with a massive frame. In PPR he's frustrating — 47 catches won't win you weeks. But in TD-heavy scoring, he's a secret weapon. At pick 3.80 he's a fine dart throw if you already have your WR1/2. Don't reach for the TD rate — it's coming down.
lane.verdict: TD-dependent red-zone specialist. Low PPR floor, high TD ceiling.

singleton.shortReport: Penn State RB with four years of split-backfield production — 1,061→752→1,099→729 rush yds, 41→362→24→158 rec yds. The receiving peak (41-362 in 2023) shows PPR upside. TD machine: 12+ rush TDs in three of four seasons.
strengths: TD machine — 12+ rushing TDs in three of four seasons (45 total)
Receiving upside shown in 2023: 41-362-1TD — can be a three-down back if given the chance
Four years of Power 5 production — experienced, reliable
Big-play ability — 6.8 YPC as a true freshman shows burst
weaknesses: Split backfield entire career — never the solo guy, can he carry a full NFL load?
Receiving production collapsed in 2024-25 (24-158, 11-72) — trending wrong
YPC declined every year: 6.8→5.2→6.5→4.5 — concerning trajectory
Lots of mileage: 634 career carries plus receiving work
singleton.nflFit: Committee back with RB2 upside if he wins a lead role. The 2023 receiving tape shows three-down potential, but recent years suggest he's a two-down runner.
singleton.comp: Jamaal Williams — reliable TD scorer, limited receiving, best in a committee
singleton.dynastyOutlook: RB3/4 with weekly RB2 upside if he lands in a TD-friendly offense. The receiving decline is worrying for PPR.
singleton.badgersFit: Singleton is a TD-dependent RB in a split backfield. The 45 career TDs are enticing, but the declining YPC and collapsing receiving production are red flags. At pick 5.165 he's a fine dart — he'll score TDs but won't give you much else. Best as an RB3/flex play. The 2023 receiving tape (41 catches) is a mirage at this point.
singleton.verdict: TD scorer with declining efficiency. RB3 floor, RB2 ceiling in the right spot.

fields.shortReport: Grad transfer WR with three straight 800+ yd seasons — 55-811-5 (UVA), 55-808-7 (UVA), 56-805-6 (ND). Remarkably consistent. Not flashy but reliable. 14.4-14.7 YPR shows downfield ability.
strengths: Three straight 800+ yd seasons — remarkable consistency across two programs
14.4-14.7 YPR — wins downfield with tracking and body control
Grad transfer to ND and didn't miss a beat — NFL-ready preparation
Reliable hands — drops are rare, catches in traffic consistently
weaknesses: Not sudden or explosive — wins with technique and size, not burst
Older prospect — grad transfer means he's already 23-24
Never had a true breakout — 800 yds is good not great
TD production modest (5-7 per year) — limited red-zone upside
fields.nflFit: Possession Z-receiver in a timing-based offense. His consistency is his calling card — he'll be a reliable NFL starter but never a star.
fields.comp: Allen Robinson — consistent, reliable, not explosive
fields.dynastyOutlook: WR4/5 with a safe floor. The consistency is appealing but the ceiling is capped. Older prospect reduces long-term upside.
fields.badgersFit: Fields is the definition of steady — 800+ yds every year, never spectacular, never invisible. At pick 3.74 he's a solid WR4/5 who won't tank your week. The age (grad transfer) limits his dynasty upside. He's a win-now piece, not a keeper. Grab him if you need reliable WR depth.
fields.verdict: Consistent possession WR. Safe floor, capped ceiling.

branch.shortReport: USC→Georgia transfer WR with elite return ability (2 KR TD, 1 PR TD in 2023) and ascending receiving production — 45→46→50 catches. Speed and elusiveness in space are his calling cards. Receiving volume is modest but the return value adds a floor.
strengths: Elite return ability — 2 KR TD and 1 PR TD in 2023, 1 KR TD in 2024
Speed and elusiveness in space — makes defenders miss in the open field
Ascending receiving production: 45→46→50 catches, 3→4→6 TDs
Versatile — contributes as a runner on jet sweeps (4-63 in 2023)
weaknesses: Modest receiving volume — 50 catches in 2025 isn't WR1 territory
Return TDs are fluky — can't count on them year over year
Smaller frame — can be erased by press coverage
USC→UGA transfer — needed a bigger stage but production didn't spike
branch.nflFit: Slot/returner in a spread offense. The return value gives him a roster floor even if the offense doesn't feature him as a receiver.
branch.comp: Devin Duvernay — return value plus slot receiving
branch.dynastyOutlook: WR5/6 with return-yard value. In standard leagues he's a fringe roster player. In return-yard leagues he's a WR4.
branch.badgersFit: Branch is a return-league asset more than a fantasy WR. The 3 return TDs in 2023 are flashy but not sustainable. At pick 3.79 he's only worth it if your league counts return yards. Otherwise, his 50-624-6 receiving line is WR5 territory. Pass in standard formats.
branch.verdict: Return-league specialist. Limited standard fantasy value.

sarratt.shortReport: JMU→Indiana transfer WR with three straight 950+ yd seasons — 67-1028-8, 53-957-8, 59-992-7. Elite YPR (15.3-18.1-16.8) shows downfield ability. Production translates across levels — G5 to P5 without missing a beat.
strengths: Three straight 950+ yd seasons — production is undeniable across two programs
Elite YPR: 15.3→18.1→16.8 — wins downfield consistently
G5→P5 translation worked — didn't regress at Indiana
8+ TDs in all three seasons — consistent red-zone contributor
weaknesses: JMU production came against lesser competition — some inflation
TDs declined from 8 to 7 despite more catches in 2025 — slight regression
Not sudden in breaks — separation against NFL corners is a question
Older prospect — multiple college seasons mean less developmental upside
sarratt.nflFit: Z-receiver in a vertical passing game. The YPR shows he can win downfield. Best with a QB who throws a great deep ball.
sarratt.comp: Darius Slayton — deep-ball specialist with consistent but modest volume
sarratt.dynastyOutlook: WR4/5 with weekly WR3 upside. The YPR is enticing but the volume ceiling is modest. Deep-league stash.
sarratt.badgersFit: Sarratt is a deep-league gem. Three straight 950+ yd seasons with 16+ YPR is rare. At pick 4.115 he's a steal if you need WR depth. The JMU-to-Indiana translation is a strong signal — he produces everywhere. He's your WR5 with WR3 upside. Don't sleep on him in the late rounds.
sarratt.verdict: Consistent deep-ball producer. Deep-league value pick.

hurst.shortReport: Georgia State WR with absurd production — 52-822-7, 67-1047-11, 74-1202-13. Sun Belt all-time receiving TD leader (31 TDs in 3 seasons). YPR over 15.5 every year. Small-school dominator who could be a late-round gem.
strengths: Sun Belt all-time receiving TD leader — 31 TDs in 3 seasons
Production climbed every year: 822→1047→1202 yds, 7→11→13 TDs
YPR over 15.5 every year — wins downfield consistently
74 catches in 2025 shows he can handle volume, not just big plays
weaknesses: Small-school competition — Sun Belt DBs aren't NFL DBs
TD rate will regress — 13 TDs on 74 catches is elite but unsustainable
Limited route tree — dominated with size and speed, not technique
Older prospect — will be 24 as a rookie
hurst.nflFit: X-receiver in a vertical offense. The production is undeniable but the competition jump is real. Best case: he's a late-round WR3. Worst case: he can't separate against NFL corners.
hurst.comp: Dominique Douglas — small-school producer who translated to a solid NFL role
hurst.dynastyOutlook: WR5/6 with WR3 upside if he lands in the right offense. The TD production is tantalizing but the competition jump is the risk.
hurst.badgersFit: Hurst is my favorite late-round dart throw. 31 TDs in 3 seasons at any level is absurd. At pick 3.84 he's worth the gamble — the production is too good to ignore. The Sun Belt competition is a fair concern, but he produced every year and got better every year. He's your WR6 with WR3 upside. In a dynasty league, this is the kind of pick that wins you a championship.
hurst.verdict: Small-school TD machine. Late-round gem if he translates.

stribling.shortReport: WSU→Ole Miss transfer WR with solid production — 63-729-4 (WSU), 54-800-6 (Ole Miss). The 2024 dip (34-488-3) with a new QB is concerning but he rebounded in 2025. Reliable possession receiver.
strengths: Reliable possession receiver — catches everything in his frame

smack.shortReport: Florida K — 22-27 FG (81.5%), long 56, 48-48 XP, 82.4% touchbacks in 2025. Lou Groza finalist. Reliable leg with NFL-caliber range.
strengths: 81.5% FG accuracy — reliable
56-yard long — NFL range
48-48 XP — perfect on extra points
82.4% touchbacks — strong leg on kickoffs
weaknesses: Kickers are kicker-dependent — production tied to NFL offense quality
5 missed FGs — not perfect
Kicker value is almost entirely landing-spot dependent
smack.nflFit: NFL kicker. Will get drafted based on leg strength and accuracy.
smack.comp: Jake Moody — reliable college kicker with NFL range
smack.dynastyOutlook: K2. Draft him if your league starts a K and you want a rookie. Landing spot determines his value entirely.
smack.badgersFit: Smack is the only K worth drafting in this class. 81.5% with a 56-yard long is NFL-caliber. At pick 7.216 he's worth a last-round pick if your league starts a K. His value is 100% landing-spot dependent — a good offense makes him a K1, a bad offense makes him unrosterable.
smack.verdict: Reliable rookie K. Landing-spot dependent value.
